description_long: This is a large, rectangular barn with a long, gently curved gable roof that extends the full length of the structure. The roof is covered in overlapping shingles, giving it a textured appearance, and the eaves overhang the walls slightly. The barn sits on a rectangular plot of green grass, and its exterior walls are lined with a series of evenly spaced vertical supports or columns, creating a colonnade effect along the longer sides. The main entrance is located at one of the shorter ends, featuring a wide opening supported by several columns, but no visible doors or windows are present on the exterior. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on maximizing interior space and providing shelter, typical of agricultural buildings. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from wood, with a weathered, grayish finish.
description: A large, rectangular barn with a gently curved gable roof, shingled exterior, and colonnaded sides, set on a grassy base. The main entrance is a wide, open portico supported by columns.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide entrance supported by multiple columns and the full length of the shingled gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, narrow profile of the barn and the repetitive vertical columns along the side.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the same colonnaded structure but no visible entrance or doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the elongated shape and the series of vertical supports.
attribute_siding_pattern: vertical board siding with evenly spaced columns
attribute_door_style: single wide open portico entrance with no visible doors, supported by multiple columns
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any side
attribute_architectural_style: traditional agricultural barn with colonnaded sides
